text.title: Buyer Interview Script for Freelancers Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Buyer Interview Script for Freelancers plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The script MUST elicit the buyer's last hiring trigger (event + date + decision-maker) within the first 15 minutes; scripts without trigger elicitation MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Buyer hiring triggers are the leading indicator of when freelancers can land work; without this the interview is generic advice-fishing.

rule.statement: The script MUST surface the named budget owner (or 'no budget'); declined-to-answer responses MUST be logged as unverified.
rule.rationale: Unnamed budget authority leads to proposals routed to the wrong person; explicit elicitation prevents wasted-pitch cycles.

rule.statement: The script MUST capture vendor-preference patterns (referrals / RFP / inbound search) for the last 3 freelance hires; one-hire generalisations MUST be flagged.
rule.rationale: Single-hire data overweights an outlier engagement; 3-hire patterns reveal the channel reality.

rule.statement: Switching-pain claims MUST be evidenced with concrete cost (dollars, hours, deals lost) or labelled hypothetical; uncosted pain MUST be re-asked.
rule.rationale: Hypothetical switching-pain inflates willingness-to-pay; only costed pain predicts real switches.

rule.statement: The freelancer MUST NOT pitch their service during the interview; pitch contamination MUST cause the transcript to be excluded from synthesis.
rule.rationale: Pitching turns the interview into a sales call and corrupts the response distribution; the no-pitch rule is the protocol's integrity gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
